RQ1.question: Can operational features alone predict ICE train stop delays?
RQ1.method: Train baseline models using only railway features.

RQ2.question: Does adding historical weather data improve delay prediction?
RQ2.method: Compare models with vs without weather features (ablation).

RQ3.question: Which weather variables are most associated with ICE delays?
RQ3.method: EDA correlations + feature importance / SHAP in final models.

derivation.canceled_rule: is_canceled == True rows excluded in Notebook 03 (documented there)

classification.threshold_justification: 6 minutes aligns with Deutsche Bahn significant-delay reporting. Final value validated in Notebook 05 EDA.
classification.metric_rationale: ICE stops are mostly on-time (class imbalance). F1 and ROC-AUC are more informative than accuracy alone.

regression.target_filter: Non-canceled stops; delay can be negative (early) or positive (late).
regression.metric_rationale: MAE is interpretable in minutes. RMSE penalizes large prediction errors (severe delays).

H1.statement: Higher hourly precipitation increases ICE delay probability.
H1.test: Compare delay rate across precipitation bins in EDA; check weather feature importance in classifier.

H2.statement: Higher wind gusts increase expected delay magnitude.

H3.statement: Models with weather features outperform operational-only baselines.
H3.test: Ablation study: same algorithm, with vs without weather columns.

evaluation_protocol.train_test_split: time-based (no random shuffle) — implemented in Notebook 07
evaluation_protocol.baseline_requirement: Must beat naive baseline before using advanced models

weather_ablation.description: Train identical model twice: with and without weather features

alternatives_considered.delay_threshold_5_min: Rejected as default — more noise from minor timing differences
alternatives_considered.delay_threshold_15_min: Rejected — too few positive cases for classification
